metadata.archetype_justification: CLI tool for deploying Lambda functions via imperative AWS SDK calls. No HTTP server, no persistent data store, no auth surface, no write API.
metadata.context: Node.js CLI for deploying AWS Lambda functions.
metadata.override_rationale: Service archetype is stateless-utility and all 5 surface flags are false. Library N/A mapping applied per Step 1.5.

findings.title: CLI tool with no REST/GraphQL/AsyncAPI interface
findings.description: This CLI tool exposes 4 commands (setup, run, package, deploy) via the commander library. There is no REST, GraphQL, or AsyncAPI interface. The CLI is invoked via shell execution, not HTTP calls.
findings.gap: No network API surface exists — this is by design for a CLI tool.
findings.recommendation: If agent invocation is planned, document CLI arguments in a machine-readable format (e.g., JSON schema of CLI options).

findings.title: No HTTP/RPC surface — machine-readable spec not applicable
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — machine-readable spec is not applicable. The CLI interface is defined via commander option declarations.

findings.title: No HTTP/RPC surface — structured error responses not applicable
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — structured error responses are not applicable. The CLI uses process.exitCode (0, 1, 254, 255) and console output.

findings.title: Write operations largely idempotent via AWS SDK patterns
findings.description: The CLI performs write operations against AWS (Lambda create/update, S3 putObject) which are largely idempotent by nature. Agent_scope is read-only so this is informational.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations, so idempotency is informational only.

findings.title: Unstructured console.log output
findings.description: CLI output is unstructured console.log text mixed with JSON-stringified AWS responses. No consistent machine-parseable output envelope.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding a --json output flag for machine consumption if agent invocation is planned.

findings.title: Internal async polling for deploy completion
findings.description: The deploy command polls for Lambda LastUpdateStatus completion internally (up to 10 iterations with 3s delays). The CLI blocks until done.
findings.gap: No external async pattern needed for CLI invocation

findings.title: No event emission — results logged to stdout
findings.description: The CLI does not emit events or webhooks. It logs results to stdout upon completion.

findings.title: No API surface to rate-limit
findings.description: No API surface to rate-limit. The CLI is subject to AWS API throttling on the AWS side, handled by SDK retry logic.

findings.title: Authentication delegated to AWS IAM credential chain
findings.description: The CLI consumes AWS credentials (access key/secret, profile, session token) from the caller. It does not issue or validate identities itself — authentication is delegated entirely to the AWS SDK.
findings.gap: No gap — architecturally correct for a CLI tool.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Agents would authenticate via IAM roles/profiles passed as CLI arguments or environment variables.

findings.title: Permission scoping delegated to IAM policies
findings.description: The CLI operates under whatever IAM permissions the caller provides. It does not enforce permission scoping — that responsibility belongs to IAM policy attached to the caller's credentials.
findings.gap: No minimum IAM policy documented for each CLI command
findings.recommendation: Document minimum IAM policy required for each CLI command.

findings.title: Action-level authorization via IAM
findings.description: The CLI does not enforce action-level authorization. Separate CLI commands naturally map to different IAM permission sets. Authorization is delegated to AWS IAM.
findings.gap: No gap — IAM provides adequate action-level authorization.

findings.title: Identity propagation not applicable for stateless-utility
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface and stateless-utility archetype — identity propagation is not applicable. The CLI uses provided credentials directly against AWS APIs.

findings.title: Credentials managed via AWS credential provider chain
findings.description: Credentials sourced from environment variables, AWS profiles, or CLI arguments. Supports AWS credential provider chain. No hardcoded production credentials. Example files contain placeholders only.
findings.gap: No gap — appropriate for a CLI tool

findings.title: Audit logging at AWS CloudTrail layer
findings.description: System does not execute agent-invoked write operations — audit logging is a consumer responsibility. AWS CloudTrail audits all API calls made by this CLI.
findings.gap: N/A — audit exists at the AWS platform layer

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND surface-flag downgrade (has_auth_surface=false, has_write_operations=false)
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: System does not execute agent-invoked write operations. Audit logging is a consumer responsibility. The CLI's AWS API calls are audited by CloudTrail on the AWS account side.

findings.title: Agent identity suspension via IAM credential revocation
findings.description: System does not issue or enforce agent identities — suspension is a consumer responsibility. Managed via IAM credential revocation.

findings.title: No write operations — compensation not applicable
findings.description: System exposes no write operations of its own. Lambda versioning/aliases provide rollback at the AWS layer.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND surface-flag downgrade (has_write_operations=false, has_http_rpc_surface=false)
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: System exposes no write operations — compensation logic is not applicable. The CLI deploys to AWS Lambda which supports versioning and aliases for rollback at the AWS service layer.

findings.title: No queryable state — AWS APIs provide function state
findings.description: CLI does not expose queryable state. AWS APIs provide current state for deployed functions.

findings.title: Concurrency controls via AWS Lambda update serialization
findings.description: No internal concurrency controls. AWS Lambda's update serialization provides adequate protection.

findings.title: AWS SDK built-in retry provides adequate resilience
findings.description: Uses AWS SDK built-in retry logic. No explicit circuit breaker library — appropriate for a CLI tool that exits after each invocation.

findings.title: No HTTP/RPC surface — rate limiting not applicable
findings.description: System exposes no HTTP/RPC surface — API-layer rate limiting is not applicable. AWS API throttling applies on the AWS side.

findings.title: No transaction limits — read-only scope
findings.description: No configurable transaction limits. Multi-region deploy controlled by comma-separated region parameter.

findings.title: CLI tool — no infrastructure to size
findings.description: CLI tool, not a running service. No infrastructure to size. Capacity concerns apply to the AWS APIs being called.

findings.title: No draft/pending state — package command provides natural staging
findings.description: No draft/pending state concept. The package command creates a local zip file (staging) before deploy provides a natural approval gate.

findings.title: No approval gates — CLI executes immediately
findings.description: No approval gates configured. CLI executes immediately upon invocation. Approval gates would be at the orchestration layer.

findings.title: Environment separation via --environment flag
findings.description: Library/utility — does not own staging environments. Supports --environment flag for environment separation via function naming convention.

findings.title: Not a data-handling target
findings.description: Not a data-handling target — no PII/PHI/financial/credential data is stored, processed, or logged by this tool.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: dev-library-application override applied
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: CLI tool classified as dev-library-application. Does not store, process, or log PII/PHI/financial/credential data.

b1.reasoning: Stage A = No. Not a data-handling target.

findings.title: No persistent data store — residency not applicable
findings.description: No persistent data store and no user-data logging — residency requirements do not apply. The CLI deploys to user-specified AWS regions but holds no data itself.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND surface-flag downgrade (has_persistent_data_store=false, has_logging_of_user_data=false)
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: No persistent data store and no user-data logging — residency requirements do not apply to this CLI tool.

findings.title: No data query surface
findings.description: No data query surface. The CLI performs deployment operations, not data retrieval.

findings.title: Not a system of record
findings.description: Not a system of record for any data. AWS Lambda service is the SoR for function configurations.

findings.title: No persistent data store — temporal metadata not applicable
findings.description: No persistent data store — temporal metadata is not applicable. The CLI operates on current configuration files at invocation time.

findings.title: No user data logged — PII risk not applicable
findings.description: System does not log user data and holds no user data — PII-in-logs risk is not applicable.

findings.title: No data sets managed — quality metrics not applicable
findings.description: No data sets managed by this tool. Quality metrics are not applicable.

findings.title: Semver versioning with no breaking change detection in CI
findings.description: Uses semver versioning (1.3.0). CLI options defined via commander. No formal breaking change detection in CI for CLI arguments.
findings.gap: No automated CLI argument stability testing
findings.recommendation: Consider adding CLI argument stability tests to CI to detect breaking changes in the command interface.

findings.title: Clear, self-documenting CLI option names
findings.description: CLI options use clear, self-documenting names (--functionName, --memorySize, --timeout, --region). No legacy codes or abbreviations.

findings.title: No data catalog — not applicable for CLI tool
findings.description: No data catalog or metadata layer. Not applicable for a CLI deployment tool.

findings.title: X-Ray SDK integrated for local Lambda execution tracing
findings.description: Library/utility — tracing and correlation are consumer concerns. The CLI integrates AWS X-Ray SDK for local Lambda handler execution tracing.
findings.gap: N/A — library obligation is trace propagation which X-Ray integration satisfies

findings.title: Alerting is a consumer concern for CLI tools
findings.description: Library/utility — alerting on error rates and latency is a consumer concern. CLI provides exit codes for orchestration-layer monitoring.

findings.title: No custom business metrics — success/failure to stdout
findings.description: No custom business metrics published. Success/failure reported to stdout.

evaluations.reason: Application repository with library N/A mapping applied (dev-library-application override). This question does not apply.
